zh.lifeSummary: 當Daniel Thomas於大約1645年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文時，他的父親John Thomas31歲，母親Tabitha29歲。他於1669年2月3日與Rebecca Thompson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1694年2月2日在他的家鄉享年50歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文特 邱奇 奧 馮 格林 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Daniel Thomas于大约1645年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文时，他的父亲John Thomas31岁，母亲Tabitha29岁。他于1669年2月3日与Rebecca Thompson在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1694年2月2日在他的家乡享年50岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文特 邱奇 奧 馮 格林 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Daniel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘God is my judge’ in Hebrew), borne by the prophet whose story is told in the Book of Daniel. He was an Israelite slave of the Assyrian king Nebuchadnezzar, who obtained great favour through his skill in interpreting dreams and the ‘writing on the wall’ at the feast held by Nebuchadnezzar's son Belshazzar. His enemies managed to get him cast into a lions' den, but he was saved by God. This was a favourite tale in the Middle Ages, often represented in miracle plays. The name has been perennially popular among English speakers since the 16th century and has been particularly favoured since the 1980s.

variants.name: Danyal
meanings: Fanciful respelling of   Daniel .

historicEvents.description: New Haven County was created on 10 May 1666, and was named for an English port town called Newhaven, London, England

en.lifeSummary: When Daniel Thomas was born about 1645, in New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, his father, John Thomas, was 31 and his mother, Tabitha, was 29. He married Rebecca Thompson on 3 February 1669, in New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 2 February 1694, in his hometown, at the age of 50, and was buried in Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

Il a épousé Rebecca Thompson le 3 février 1669, à New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 2 février 1694, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, États-Unis.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Daniel Thomas è nato il circa 1645 nel New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, il suo padre, John Thomas, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Tabitha, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Rebecca Thompson il 3 febbraio 1669, nel New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 2 febbraio 1694, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Daniel Thomasが約1645年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンで生まれた時，父John Thomasは31歳，母Tabithaは29歳であった。1669年2月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンでRebecca Thompsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1694年2月2日に故郷で50歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニュー ヘイベンセンター チャーチ オン ス グレン チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Daniel Thomas(이)가 대략 1645년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Thomas(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Tabitha(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1669년 2월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐에서 Rebecca Thompson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1694년 2월 2일 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴 하븐 세터 c후르치  테 그린 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Daniel Thomas nasceu aproximadamente 1645, em New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, seu pai, John Thomas, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Tabitha, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Rebecca Thompson em 3 de fevereiro de 1669, em New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1694, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Daniel Thomas родился около 1645 г. в местности ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени John Thomas было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Tabitha, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Rebecca Thompson 3 февраля 1669 г. в местности ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 2 февраля 1694 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности кентир чурч он т грин чурчярд, ню хавен, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Daniel Thomas nació aproximadamente 1645, en New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, su papá, John Thomas, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Tabitha, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Rebecca Thompson el 3 de febrero de 1669, en New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 2 de febrero de 1694, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
